name: Claude Code Hooks
description: Claude Code lifecycle hooks that capture tool-use turns and inject recalled memory context into prompts, plus the fire-and-forget event emit and stdout-discipline helpers that keep hooks non-blocking.

name: Orchestration
description: The iii worker engine that registers queue functions (ingestTurn, flushSession, sweepIdle) and cron triggers, plus the driver scripts that exercise the turn-event flow end to end.

name: Write Pipeline
description: The asynchronous write path that gates turns by salience, buffers session windows, extracts candidate memories via the LLM, resolves entities and conflicts, and persists memories with supersede/extends links.

name: Read Pipeline
description: The synchronous read path that plans a query, runs hybrid recall, ranks candidates within a token budget, and formats the injected context header surfaced back to the session.

name: Matching & Maintenance
description: The reusable matching engine and similarity scorers used for entity resolution, plus the background maintenance sweeps that decay, forget, expire memories and repair duplicate entities.

name: Persistence & Foundation
description: The HelixDB access layer (client, schema, entity and memory persistence, hybrid retrieval, bootstrap) together with the LLM completion/embedding clients and the central environment configuration the whole pipeline depends on.

name: Interfaces
description: The UniMind MCP server that exposes the recall and remember tools over stdio to Claude Code.

name: Infrastructure & Config
description: Container and orchestration definitions (Dockerfile, docker-compose services, entrypoint), HelixDB and build configuration, and project tooling files that deploy and configure the system.

name: Documentation & Experiments
description: System design and workflow documentation plus the offline drift-gate experiment that evaluates topic-shift detection heuristics against labeled sessions.
